title: wyred contract v0 — layer-2 canonical netlist (.l2.json)
description: Descriptive schema for the layer-2 CanonicalGraph netlist, extracted from harness schema.to_json (precedence: goldens > harness > EMIT_CONTRACT Part A prose). The top-level object has EXACTLY the four keys components/nets/escalations/invariants, all always emitted — schema.to_json emits NO top-level 'layer' key (unlike l1). Terminals emit all seven keys unconditionally (req_v/prov_v/iface/iface_member nullable); nets emit ground_kind/ground_role/bond ONLY when set. Closed vocabularies as enums: TERMINAL_ROLES, NET_KINDS, LOGIC_FNS (plus null), GROUND_KINDS, GROUND_ROLES, INVARIANT_KINDS; Component.kind and every iface are OPEN per finding F2. Findings encoded: F3 (voltage number|null on EVERY net kind — ground nets carry 0.0), F7 (escalations is a plain STRING array here), F9 (nodes are [refdes, terminal] 2-tuples). additionalProperties:false everywhere except the free attrs dict.

escalations.description: F7: layer-2 escalations are plain strings (harness CanonicalGraph.escalations: List[str]); the l1/records escalations field is an array of rich objects instead.

attrs.description: free-form salient-property dict (e.g. i2c_addr, l1_role, for_demand provenance); the one open shape

node.description: F9: an (refdes, terminal_name) pair — exactly two strings. In l2 nets/invariants nodes are these 2-tuples; the alloc-wiring and pinmap data paths encode the same thing as a dotted "REFDES.PIN" string instead (see those schemas in Step 1.1b).

terminal.description: A connection point / pin (schema._terminal_to_json). ALL SEVEN keys always emitted (no emit-only-when-set on terminals); req_v/prov_v/iface/iface_member are nullable. role is the closed TERMINAL_ROLES vocabulary; iface is OPEN (F2); function is a human label nothing keys off.

component.description: A single instance (schema._component_to_json). ALL SEVEN keys always emitted; logic_fn is nullable and, when set, one of LOGIC_FNS. kind is OPEN vocabulary (F2: 35 kinds observed across ga019 l2 goldens vs 12 declared in schema.COMPONENT_KINDS).

kind.description: OPEN string (F2), NOT enum-constrained. Non-normative observed vocabulary: the 35 distinct kinds present across the ga019 l2 goldens (derived mechanically from the corpus, not hand-listed): antenna, buck_regulator, can_transceiver, capacitor, charger, connector, crystal, current_sense, diode, fiducial, flash, fuse, fuse_holder, gate_driver, inductor, jumper, ldo, led, logic_gate, mcu, mechanical, mosfet, motor, resistor, resonator, sensor, source, supervisor, switch, test_point, thermistor, transistor, uart_device, usb_protection, varistor. Only 12 of these are declared in schema.COMPONENT_KINDS; the rest are producer-emitted but undeclared. Consumers MUST accept kinds outside this list.

net.description: A logical node / net (schema._net_to_json). name/kind/voltage/nodes always emitted; ground_kind/ground_role/bond emit-only-when-set (backward-compatible v3 fields). kind is the closed NET_KINDS vocabulary. F3: voltage is number|null on EVERY kind — ground nets carry voltage 0.0 across all goldens (and in prose worked example D.3), contradicting Part A's 'else None' field-table text; the schema follows the goldens.

invariant.description: A layer-2 structural safety invariant over concrete nodes (schema._invariant_to_json). kind/a/b/inputs always emitted; a/b are single (refdes, terminal) node pairs and inputs an array of such pairs. kind is the closed INVARIANT_KINDS vocabulary. NOTE: distinct from the layer-1 invariant_decl (l1.schema.json) whose subjects are intent-level string references.
